title: abutilon whistleri
intro: Abutilon whistleri is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant native to the island of Rarotonga in the Cook Islands. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Abutilon whistleri are said to include treating fever, sore throats, and skin conditions, as well as providing antioxidant and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or by using the roots in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
